steps.title: Find the decisive clue
steps.content: Velázquez is introduced as the Spanish king's court painter. Then comes “but”—a contrast marker—followed by “painters around the world” employing his techniques. The strongest clue is that phrase about global influence: the blank must describe a geographic limitation that this contradicts.

steps.title: Apply the clue
steps.content: ”Confined to” means “limited to”—his influence was hardly limited to Spain. A and D don't address geographic scope, and B contradicts the opening. This perfectly captures the “but”: despite being court-bound, his techniques spread globally. That matches choice C — C is correct.
